steps.title: Fit the two given account balances
steps.content: This is a perfect Desmos custom-regression problem. The account starts at 35,600 dollars, so the point $(0,35600)$ is on the exponential model. After 15 years, the balance is 129,672.38 dollars, so $(15,129672.38)$ is also on the model. Put those two points in a Desmos table and fit $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$.

steps.title: Read the regression output
steps.content: Desmos returns $a\approx35600$ and $b\approx1.09$. In the model $y=a b^x$, $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the annual multiplier. That gives $$A(t)=35600(1.09)^t.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A has the fitted starting value $35600$ and multiplier $1.09$. Choice B uses $0.09$, which would make the balance shrink almost to zero. Choices C and D use the wrong starting value, because $A(0)$ must be 35,600 dollars. Choice A is correct.
